name: Valdesalor Reservoir Loop from Aldea del Cano
name_original: Embalse de Valdesalor desde Aldea del Cano – circular por el río Salor
description: Cycle 23.3 miles (37.5 km) around Embalse de Valdesalor from Aldea del Cano, exploring historical bridges and diverse natural scenery.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Aldea Del Cano navigate a landscape characterized by open countryside and several significant reservoirs. The region features rolling terrain with moderate elevation changes, offering varied cycling experiences. Paths often lead past water bodies like the Valdesalor and Proserpina Reservoirs, providing scenic views. This area is suitable for cyclists seeking routes that combine natural scenery with manageable climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Aldea Del Cano The most popular touring cycling route is Valdesalor Reservoir Loop from Aldea del Cano, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Valdesalor Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Proserpina Reservoir Route – Roman Dam of Proserpina loop from Casas de Don Antonio, a difficult 79.5 miles (128.0 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain and expansive reservoir scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Santiago de Bencáliz Bridge – Puente Mocha loop from Dehesa de Menudos, a 50.7 miles (81.6 km) trail leading through varied countryside, often completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Aldea Del Cano is defined by open countryside, reservoir views, and historical bridges. The network offers options for easy touring bike trails as well as more advanced touring bike routes. The routes in Aldea Del Cano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aldea Del Cano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aldea Del Cano?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes in the Aldea Del Cano area, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike routes in Aldea Del Cano?
A: The region features open countryside with rolling terrain and moderate elevation changes. You'll often find paths leading past significant water bodies like the Valdesalor and Proserpina Reservoirs, providing scenic views and varied cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any easy touring bike trails suitable for beginners in Aldea Del Cano?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy touring bike trail available. The region offers options for cyclists seeking routes with manageable climbs, making it suitable for those newer to touring cycling.

Q: Are there any challenging touring bike routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult touring bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Reservoir Route – Roman Dam of Proserpina loop from Casas de Don Antonio&lt;/a&gt;, which is 79.5 miles (128.0 km) long with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocha&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Bencáliz Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Las Arguijuelas de Abajo&lt;/a&gt; and sections through the Extremaduran dehesa.

Q: Are there many loop routes for touring cyclists in Aldea Del Cano?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Aldea Del Cano are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103157446/valdesalor-reservoir-loop-from-aldea-del-cano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdesalor Reservoir Loop from Aldea del Cano&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garden Next to the Cemetery loop from Albalá&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103157446/valdesalor-reservoir-loop-from-aldea-del-cano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdesalor Reservoir Loop from Aldea del Cano&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours, while longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Bencáliz Bridge – Puente Mocha loop from Dehesa de Menudos&lt;/a&gt; can take over 6 hours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Aldea Del Cano?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open countryside, the scenic reservoir views, and the historical bridges that define the routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Jarripa Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garden Next to the Cemetery – Jarripa Reservoir loop from Casas de Don Antonio&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that will take you past the Jarripa Reservoir, offering beautiful water views.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling around Aldea Del Cano?
A: While specific data isn't available, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in this region of Spain, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat than summer.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: While there isn't a specific 'family-friendly' designation, the 1 easy route available would likely be the most suitable option for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride.
tips: 15th century castle of great patrimonial and cultural importance. Today, the castle and surrounding gardens serve as a hotel and event space.
Roman bridge over the river Salor on the old Vía de la Plata and later on the Camino de Santiago.
Defensive castle from the 15th century. Its stone tower can be seen for miles in the surrounding countryside.
Stone bridge over the Salor river on the route to Torreorgaz. On the south shore you can see the Hermitage of San Isidro. In the dry season the water levels are very low.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 37.5 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 56 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires a good level of fitness, especially due to the varied terrain which includes unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. While some parts may be paved, a significant portion consists of 'ground' and gravel tracks. Cyclists should be prepared for potentially rough or loose surfaces, making a touring bike with wider, robust tires ideal.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain, which includes unpaved ground and gravel tracks, a touring bicycle with wider, more robust tires is highly recommended. This will provide better comfort and stability than a standard road bike.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical elements. You'll cycle alongside the Valdesalor reservoir and the Salor River, passing through Mediterranean forests. Notable historical sights include the Castillo de Las Arguijuelas de Abajo, the La Mocha Bridge (Salor River), the Ruins of Castillo del Cachorro, and a Stone Bridge over the Salor River, some of which are ancient Roman structures.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable during various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Winter can be a good time for birdwatching around the Embalse de Valdesalor, as many aquatic bird species are present.

faqs.question: What wildlife can be spotted along the Embalse de Valdesalor?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Valdesalor is an important ecological corridor and a significant area for birdwatching. You can expect to see numerous species of aquatic birds, especially during the winter months.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for parking in Aldea del Cano, the starting point of this circular route. Public parking options are usually available in the village center.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for cycling routes can vary. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in rural areas, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and to check local regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Natural Corredor Cáceres-Badajoz, the Cañada Real del Puerto del Pico, and the Mtb Vía de la Plata.
